records.title: Understand Dutch higher education
records.description: Dutch higher education includes professionally oriented HBO at universities of applied sciences and research-oriented WO at universities, with associate, bachelor, master and doctoral qualifications.
records.ai_summary: Compare learning style, entry requirements, study load and career goal rather than translating every institution as a university. HBO and WO can lead to degrees at the same framework level but have different orientation.

records.title: Compare HBO and WO study orientation
records.description: The Dutch system distinguishes practice-oriented higher professional education from theory and research-oriented university education, with different programme structures and typical admission routes.
records.ai_summary: Review programme content, internships, assessment and progression rather than choosing by name alone. Ask the institution how a prior diploma fits its own admission decision.

records.title: Check whether an institution and programme are recognised
records.description: Recognition and accreditation should be verified before enrolment, particularly for private providers or programmes marketed internationally, because funding and degree status can differ.

official_source.title: Educational institutions and study programmes
official_source.publisher: National Commission Code of Conduct Higher Education

records.ai_summary: Confirm both the institution and the exact programme, location and study mode in official registers. Marketing language alone is not proof that a programme is accredited or government funded.

records.title: Compare Dutch diploma and qualification levels
records.description: Dutch associate, bachelor, master and doctoral awards map to defined NLQF and EQF levels, while the framework level describes learning outcomes rather than identical content or professional rights.
records.ai_summary: Use qualification levels as a comparison tool, then check programme content and admission or professional requirements separately. Equal framework levels do not make two qualifications interchangeable.

records.title: Apply to study with a foreign diploma
records.description: Applicants with foreign qualifications should check the institution's entry requirements and may need translations or a credential evaluation, but the institution makes the final admission decision.
records.ai_summary: Ask the programme which diploma, transcript, language and translation evidence it accepts before ordering evaluations. Submit documents early enough for both central enrolment and institutional review.

records.title: Understand higher-education credential evaluation
records.description: A credential evaluation compares a foreign qualification with Dutch levels and is advisory; it supports understanding but does not itself guarantee admission, employment or professional recognition.
records.ai_summary: First establish whether an institution or authority actually requires an evaluation. Prepare complete diplomas, transcripts and translations and distinguish academic admission from regulated-profession recognition.

records.title: Use Studielink for higher-education enrolment
records.description: Studielink is the central application and enrolment service for many Dutch higher-education programmes, used together with institution-specific portals, documents and selection steps.
records.ai_summary: Create one accurate account, select the exact programme and start date, and monitor both Studielink and institution messages. A Studielink request alone may not complete admission or enrolment.

records.title: Plan an international higher-education application
records.description: International applicants must coordinate programme research, admission requirements, language evidence, deadlines, finances, housing and any immigration steps with the chosen institution.
records.ai_summary: Build a deadline list from the programme's official page and distinguish application, document, selection, visa and enrolment dates. Keep institution confirmations and do not rely on a generic national deadline.

records.title: Verify higher-education accreditation
records.description: NVAO explains the Dutch higher-education system, accreditation and the official CROHO register, helping applicants verify whether a programme and institution are officially recognised.
records.ai_summary: Use the NVAO guidance to identify the applicable Dutch accreditation route and follow the official CROHO register reference. Match the exact programme, institution, location and study variant before paying or enrolling.

records.title: Check protections for international students
records.description: Institutions that participate in the Code of Conduct for International Students commit to standards on recruitment, admission, information and services for students from abroad.

official_source.title: Code of Conduct for international students

records.ai_summary: Check whether the institution is listed under the current code and compare promised services with official admission information. The code complements, but does not replace, programme accreditation checks.
